spans.content: Zhiqi Bu, Yu-Xiang Wang,Sheng ZhaGeorge Karypis
Per-example gradient clipping is a key algorithmic step that enables practical
differential private (DP) training for deep learning models.Thechoice of
is shown to be vital for achieving high accuracy under
DP but difficult to tune (must be tuned jointly with learning rate).
results.Left:GPT2 on E2E dataset using DP-AdamW.Right:ResNet18on ImageNet
We propose an easy-to-use replacement,called Automatic Clipping, that
eliminates theneed to tune R forany DPoptimizers,while beingas private
and computationally efficient as existing DP optimizers.
·We propose a new per-sample gradient clipping function does not re-
deep learningas easy as the regular non-private one.
·We give a convergence analysis of automatic DP-SGD in the non-
convex seting,which shows that it enjoys anasymptotic convergence
that matches the standard non-private SGD.
